title: Collection from Wastewater Treatment Plant, Transportation, and Storage of Raw Wastewater
author: Kevin Calci
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bycepste

steps.title: If samples cannot be processed immediately,...
steps.instruction: If samples cannot be processed immediately, samples should be stored at 2-8 °C for up to 7 days.

steps.title: Composite samples can be collected as flow-paced or time-paced
steps.instruction: Composite samples can be collected as flow-paced or time-paced. Follow manufacturer's instructions for proper programming of the ISCO and then initiate collection. If using the WWTP’s automated sampler, be aware that they are generally not set up for aseptic sampling. It is strongly recommended to discuss sampling requirements with facility management prior to initiation of any sampling.

steps.title: Ensure suction line of the ISCO...
steps.instruction: Ensure suction line of the ISCO is placed downstream in the headworks, preferably after screener and de-gritting. Generally, the weighted suction strainer should but placed in an area of high hydrological turbulence and not touching the bottom or sides of influent channel (as described above in "before start" section).

steps.title: Ensure adequate headspace (at least 3-4...
steps.instruction: Ensure adequate headspace (at least 3-4 cm) exists in the sample container to allow for complete mixing prior to analysis.

steps.title: Grab samples can be collected using...
steps.instruction: Grab samples can be collected using a peristaltic pump (section 3.1) or a dip pole (section 3.2). Ensure sample is collected from a consistent location with adequate mixing to be representative of the wastewater. An automated sampler (e.g., ISCO) can be used as the peristaltic pump, if available.

steps.title: When possible, new sample containers should be used
steps.instruction: When possible, new sample containers should be used. If not feasible, sample containers should be clean and autoclave sterilized prior to collection.

steps.title: Ensure sample container(s) are labeled with...
steps.instruction: Ensure sample container(s) are labeled with indelible marker or waterproof labeling.

steps.title: When using a peristaltic pump, place...
steps.instruction: When using a peristaltic pump, place end of suction line into appropriate collection area (as described above in "before start" section). Start pump. Purge collection tubing by directing the tubing outlet to flow into the influent waste stream until approximately a litter flushes the tubing. Shut pump off. Move collection tubing outlet to the sample container and turn on pump to deliver intended sample volume. Shut pump off and cap the bottle tightly. Run pump in reverse direction to empty the collection tubing, paying close attention to facilitate all the influent making its way out of the tubing.

steps.title: When using a dip pole, ensure...
steps.instruction: When using a dip pole, ensure the collection bottle on the pole is clean and securely fastened to the pole. Dip the collection bottle into the appropriate collection area (as described above in "before start" section) to rinse at least three times with the influent waste stream. Collect sample. Transfer the collected wastewater into the sample container. Do not collect sample directly in sample container.

steps.title: When possible, new sample containers should be used
steps.instruction: When possible, new sample containers should be used. If not feasible, sample containers should be clean and autoclave sterilized prior to collection.

steps.title: Ensure sample container(s) are labeled with...
steps.instruction: Ensure sample container(s) are labeled with indelible marker or waterproof labeling.

steps.title: Fill base of ISCO with ice...
steps.instruction: Fill base of ISCO with ice and secure collection bottles in proper orientation. Add ice to remaining space around the collection bottles to ensure sample(s) remain cool from collection to transport, as necessary. If available, include temperature recording devices (Smart Buttons) to monitor sample temperature.

steps.title: After appropriate time, return to WWTP...
steps.instruction: After appropriate time, return to WWTP to collect filled sample bottles. If sample bottle(s) are overfilled, do not pour out sample. Allow transfer to a larger vessel for adequate mixing at the laboratory. Re-calibrate the ISCO for collection volume prior to subsequent collections.

steps.title: Place wastewater samples in secondary containment...
steps.instruction: Place wastewater samples in secondary containment (polyethylene bag) and then place on wet ice in an insulated container (i.e., cooler). When possible, transport bottles in a rack to ensure they remain upright.

steps.title: Ensure wastewater sample bottles are tightly...
steps.instruction: Ensure wastewater sample bottles are tightly sealed and decontaminate the surface with a bleach-based disinfectant (i.e., Hype-Wipes).

steps.title: Ensure adequate ice is present to...
steps.instruction: Ensure adequate ice is present to secure the bottles in the transport container and maintain sample temperature below 8 °C for the duration of transportation. If samples are to be shipped by commercial carrier, adhere to appropriate packaging and labeling requirements of IATA regulations for biological substances.

steps.title: If samples need to be stored...
steps.instruction: If samples need to be stored longer than 7 days, frozen storage at -70 °C or -20 °C is recommended.

steps.title: Follow manufacturer's instructions to calibrate the...
steps.instruction: Follow manufacturer's instructions to calibrate the collection volume of the ISCO to ensure adequate headspace (3-4 cm) will exist in the sample bottle(s) to allow for complete mixing prior to analysis in the laboratory. Calibration should not need to be done on each sampling occassion.
